result:
Now I have the full picture. Here's the answer.

## Note on file location
`src/drivers/driver_nl80211.c` only contains a thin, error‑free wrapper. The actual scan‑trigger logic (and all the error paths) lives in the sibling translation unit **`src/drivers/driver_nl80211_scan.c`**, which is not `#include`d by `driver_nl80211.c` but is a separate compile‑DB unit in the same nl80211 driver backend. The call graph confirms this: `driver_nl80211_scan2()` (in `driver_nl80211.c:7223`) is the `.scan2` op registered in `wpa_driver_ops`, and it does nothing but call `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan()` in `driver_nl80211_scan.c:214`.

## Functions involved

### 1. `driver_nl80211_scan2()` — `driver_nl80211.c:7223`
Pure passthrough: `return wpa_driver_nl80211_scan(bss, params);` — no independent error condition; any failure is whatever the callee returns.

### 2. `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan()` — `driver_nl80211_scan.c:214` (the real scan-trigger function)
Returns `-1` (generic failure, no errno detail preserved to caller) or the negative errno from `send_and_recv_msgs()`:

- `TEST_FAIL()` returns true (line 224) — test-hook forced failure, returns `-1` immediately.
- `nl80211_scan_common(bss, NL80211_CMD_TRIGGER_SCAN, params)` returns `NULL` (line 228‑229) — message build failed (see function 4 below), returns `-1`.
- P2P-probe branch (`params->p2p_probe`, line 231‑253): any of
  - `nla_nest_start(msg, NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_SUPP_RATES)` returns `NULL`,
  - `nla_put(msg, NL80211_BAND_2GHZ, ...)` fails,
  - `nla_put_flag(msg, NL80211_ATTR_TX_NO_CCK_RATE)` fails
  → `goto fail`, frees msg, returns `ret` (still `-1`, unset by send yet).
- `send_and_recv_msgs(drv, msg, NULL, NULL)` returns non-zero (line 255‑281) — kernel/nl80211 rejected the trigger command:
  - If interface is AP mode (`drv->hostapd && is_ap_interface(drv->nlmode)`), it tries a fallback: switch to station mode and retry the scan.
    - If `wpa_driver_nl80211_set_mode(bss, NL80211_IFTYPE_STATION)` fails → `goto fail`, returns the original nonzero `ret`.
    - If the recursive `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan(bss, params)` retry also fails → restores old mode, `goto fail`, returns nonzero `ret`.
  - Otherwise (not an AP interface being worked around) → `goto fail` directly, returns the nonzero `ret` from `send_and_recv_msgs`.
- Success path (`ret == 0` or AP-mode fallback succeeded) falls through to schedule the scan-timeout via eloop and returns `0`.

### 3. `nl80211_scan_common()` — `driver_nl80211_scan.c:107` (shared scan/sched-scan message builder; returns `NULL` on failure)
Called by both `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan()` and `wpa_driver_nl80211_sched_scan()`. Returns `NULL` under any of:
- `nl80211_cmd_msg(bss, 0, cmd)` returns `NULL` (line 116‑117) — see function 4.
- `params->num_ssids` set and:
  - `nla_nest_start(msg, NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_SSIDS)` returns `NULL`, or
  - any `nla_put()` of an SSID fails (loop over `num_ssids`)
- `params->extra_ies` set and `nla_put(msg, NL80211_ATTR_IE, ...)` fails.
- `params->freqs` set and:
  - `nla_nest_start(msg, NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_FREQUENCIES)` returns `NULL`, or
  - any `nla_put_u32()` of a frequency fails (loop until `freqs[i]==0`)
- `params->mac_addr_rand` set and:
  - `params->mac_addr` set and `nla_put(msg, NL80211_ATTR_MAC, ...)` fails, or
  - `params->mac_addr_mask` set and `nla_put(msg, NL80211_ATTR_MAC_MASK, ...)` fails
- `scan_flags` non-zero and `nla_put_u32(msg, NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_FLAGS, scan_flags)` fails.

All of these `goto fail`, which does `nlmsg_free(msg); return NULL;`.

### 4. `nl80211_cmd_msg()` — `driver_nl80211.c:455` (generic genl message allocator, used by scan and many other commands)
Returns `NULL` when:
- `nlmsg_alloc()` fails (out of memory), or
- `nl80211_cmd()` (genlmsg_put) fails, or
- `nl80211_set_iface_id()` fails (fails to attach `NL80211_ATTR_WDEV` or `NL80211_ATTR_IFINDEX`, e.g. `nla_put_u64`/`nla_put_u32` failure due to buffer exhaustion).

## Why the user sees a generic "scan failed"
Every one of `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan`'s early-return paths (`TEST_FAIL`, `nl80211_scan_common` returning `NULL`, the P2P-probe `nla_put` failures) collapses to the bare `-1`, discarding the specific reason. Only the `send_and_recv_msgs()` failure path preserves the actual kernel errno (logged via `strerror(-ret)` at `driver_nl80211_scan.c:258-259`, but that's a debug log, not part of the return value seen upstream) — so any allocation/attribute-build failure upstream of the actual netlink send is indistinguishable from a kernel-side rejection once it reaches wpa_supplicant's core.
